hawkish_phrases: ongoing increases in the target range will be appropriate in order to attain a stance of monetary policy that is sufficiently restrictive
inflation remained well above the Committee's longer-run goal of 2 percent and the labor market remained very tight
a restrictive policy stance would need to be maintained until the incoming data provided confidence that inflation was on a sustained downward path to 2 percent
upside risks to the inflation outlook remained a key factor shaping the policy outlook
a policy stance that proved to be insufficiently restrictive could halt recent progress in moderating inflationary pressures
dovish_phrases: slowing the pace of rate increases at the current juncture would allow for appropriate risk management
inflation data received over the past three months showed a welcome reduction in the monthly pace of price increases
a further slowing in the pace of rate increases would better allow them to assess the economy's progress
several participants observed that some measures of financial conditions had eased over the past few months
Several of those participants observed that risks to the economic outlook were becoming more balanced
summary: The Committee unanimously voted to raise the target range 25 basis points to 4-1/2 to 4-3/4 percent, slowing from previous 50bp and 75bp hikes, with explicit forward guidance that ongoing increases will be appropriate to achieve a sufficiently restrictive stance and maintain it until inflation is on a sustained path to 2 percent.
